title: LAYER 1 — Historical Trade: Sumerian origin + Mediterranean spread
description: Sumerian Mesopotamia ~3,400 BCE — poppy tablets describe 'hul gil' (joy plant). Earliest medical use documented.

title: LAYER 1 — Historical Trade: Sumerian origin + Mediterranean spread
description: Greece — Hippocrates recommends poppy juice for pain, sleep, internal bleeding 400 BCE.

title: LAYER 1 — Historical Trade: Sumerian origin + Mediterranean spread
description: Rome — Galen formalizes opium medicine; Marcus Aurelius reportedly used it daily.

title: LAYER 1 — Historical Trade: Ottoman + Persian supply
description: Persia — medieval + modern opium production; 19th century Qajar dynasty exports to China.

title: LAYER 1 — Historical Trade: Ottoman + Persian supply
description: Istanbul — Ottoman opium state monopoly 16th–19th centuries. Smyrna (Izmir) was the main export port.

title: LAYER 1 — Historical Trade: British Opium Wars
description: Canton — British East India Company smuggles Indian opium into China against Qing prohibition. First Opium War 1839–42 forces 'unequal treaties'.

title: LAYER 1 — Historical Trade: British Opium Wars
description: Hong Kong — ceded to Britain in Treaty of Nanking 1842. Became centre of British colonial opium trade.

title: LAYER 1 — Historical Trade: British Opium Wars
description: Bengal (India) — British East India Company monopoly opium production. Forced Indian peasants to grow poppy for the China trade.

title: LAYER 1 — Historical Trade: Modern illicit production
description: Helmand Afghanistan — world's #1 illicit opium producer (~85% of global supply 2020).

title: LAYER 1 — Historical Trade: Modern illicit production
description: Myanmar Shan State (Golden Triangle) — second-largest illicit producer; ethnic armed groups control.

title: LAYER 1 — Historical Trade: Modern illicit production
description: Sinaloa Mexico — poppy cultivation for heroin; fentanyl synthesis also concentrated here.

title: LAYER 1 — Historical Trade: US Pharma Opioid Crisis
description: NYC — Purdue Pharma (Sackler family) introduces OxyContin 1996. 'Pain is the fifth vital sign' marketing campaign.

title: LAYER 1 — Historical Trade: US Pharma Opioid Crisis
description: Appalachia (West Virginia + Ohio + Kentucky) — epidemic epicentre. ~500,000+ US overdose deaths 1999–2024.

title: LAYER 1 — Historical Trade: US Pharma Opioid Crisis
description: Wuhan / Shanghai China — fentanyl precursor chemicals shipped from Chinese labs to Mexican cartels (pre-2019 crackdown).

title: LAYER 2 — Language Diffusion: 'opium' roots
description: Greek — 'opion' (juice). Source of 'opium' in all European languages.

title: LAYER 2 — Language Diffusion: 'opium' roots
description: Latin — 'opium'. Global scientific + medical term.

title: LAYER 2 — Language Diffusion: 'opium' roots
description: Persian/Arabic — 'afyūm' / 'afiūn'. Spread along Silk Road.

title: LAYER 3 — Modern Supply Chain (today)
description: Afghanistan — 85% of world illicit opium 2020. Taliban banned 2022; cultivation dropped but processing + fentanyl continue.

title: LAYER 3 — Modern Supply Chain (today)
description: Sinaloa / Guerrero Mexico — main supplier to US illicit fentanyl + heroin market.

title: LAYER 3 — Modern Supply Chain (today)
description: Detroit / Michigan — fentanyl distribution corridor into US Midwest.

title: LAYER 3 — Modern Supply Chain (today)
description: Appalachia — epicentre of US overdose crisis, ~108,000 deaths in 2023 alone (CDC).
